steps.title: Understand the structure
steps.content: The sentence uses a dash after territories to open a parenthetical list: New Granada (present-day Colombia and Panama), Venezuela, and Quito (present-day ______. A second dash is needed to close that parenthetical list before from colonial rule. Inside the list, a closing parenthesis is also needed to close the nested (present-day ___) aside. So the blank must supply both a closing parenthesis and a closing dash.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Ecuador,)': Places a comma inside the parenthesis before the closing parenthesis, which is incorrect punctuation, and still provides no dash to close the outer parenthetical. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Ecuador)': Closes the parenthesis but omits the dash entirely, so the boundary between the list and from colonial rule is unmarked. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Ecuador),': Closes the parenthesis but uses a comma instead of the required dash; a comma cannot pair with a dash to frame a supplementary element. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Ecuador)—': Closes the parenthesis and provides the dash, creating the matched-dash structure (—New Granada…Ecuador)—) that properly sets off the list. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
